records.ety: [From hydrosol an aqueous colloidal solution, confused with G. sole, soole, salt water from which salt is obtained.]

defs: A fluid mixture of a colloid and a liquid; a liquid colloidal solution or suspension.

records.ety: [F. sole, L. solea; -- so named from its flat shape. See Sole of the foot.]

defs: Any one of several species of flatfishes of the genus Solea and allied genera of the family Soleidae, especially the common European species (Solea vulgaris), which is a valuable food fish.

Any one of several American flounders somewhat resembling the true sole in form or quality, as the California sole (Lepidopsetta bilineata), the long-finned sole (Glyptocephalus zachirus), and other species.

records.ety: [AS. sole, fr. L. soolea (or rather an assumed L. sola), akin to solumround, soil, sole of the foot. Cf. Exile, Saloon, Soil earth, Sole the fish.]

defs: The bottom of the foot; hence, also, rarely, the foot itself.
quotes: The dove found no rest for the sole of her foot.
Hast wandered through the world now long a day, Yet ceasest not thy weary soles to lead.

defs: The bottom of a shoe or boot, or the piece of leather which constitutes the bottom.
quotes: The “caliga” was a military shoe, with a very thick sole, tied above the instep.

defs: The bottom or lower part of anything, or that on which anything rests in standing.
The bottom of the body of a plow; -- called also slade; also, the bottom of a furrow.
The horny substance under a horse's foot, which protects the more tender parts.
A piece of timber attached to the lower part of the rudder, to make it even with the false keel.
The seat or bottom of a mine; -- applied to horizontal veins or lodes.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Soled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Soling.]

defs: To furnish with a sole; as, to sole a shoe.

records.ety: [L. solus, or OF. sol, F. seul (fr. L. solus; cf. L. sollus whole, entire. Cf. Desolate, Solemn, Solo, Sullen.]

defs: Being or acting without another; single; individual; only.
quotes: He, be sure . . . first and last will reign Sole king.

defs: Single; unmarried; as, a feme sole.
